name: Via Verde of Campo de Cartagena and Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán - return from Fuente Álamo de Murcia
name_original: Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena y Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán — vuelta desde Fuente Álamo de Murcia
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Fuente Álamo De Murcia traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by open plains and varied hilly terrain. The region is bordered by mountain ranges such as the Sierra del Algarrobo and parts of the Carrascoy National Park, offering routes with gentle climbs. Cyclists encounter rural scenery with scrub and bush vegetation, and many routes feature well-paved surfaces. The area's proximity to the coast also provides access to scenic coastal roads. Best touring cycling routes around Fuente Álamo De Murcia The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde of Campo de Cartagena and Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán - return from Fuente Álamo de Murcia, a 33.0 miles (53.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a greenway, offering varied natural views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Águilas Marina – Road to Mazarrón loop from Las Palas, a difficult 24.0 miles (38.6 km) path. This route features coastal roads and elevation changes, providing a challenging experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Isla Plana Coastal View – Winding Road to Mazarrón loop from Mingrano, a 31.2 miles (50.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas with winding roads, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Fuente Álamo De Murcia is defined by varied topography, rural scenery, and coastal proximity. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate greenways to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Fuente Álamo De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuente Álamo De Murcia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Fuente Álamo De Murcia?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Fuente Álamo De Murcia, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy greenways to more challenging rides with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Fuente Álamo De Murcia?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll encounter open plains, varied hilly terrain, and routes bordered by mountain ranges like the Sierra del Algarrobo and parts of the Carrascoy National Park. Many routes feature well-paved surfaces, passing through rural scenery with scrub, bush, and some pine forests.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, Fuente Álamo De Murcia offers several easier routes. For a more relaxed experience, consider a greenway route such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017262305/via-verde-of-campo-de-cartagena-and-sierra-de-la-muela-cabo-tinoso-and-roldan-return-from-fuente-alamo-de-murcia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde of Campo de Cartagena and Sierra de la Muela&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a dedicated cycle path and offers varied natural views without extreme climbs.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31416090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Águilas Marina – Road to Mazarrón loop from Las Palas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38236925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Plana Coastal View – Winding Road to Mazarrón loop from Mingrano&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and coastal roads, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Fuente Álamo De Murcia?
A: Fuente Álamo De Murcia benefits from a year-round pleasant climate, making it suitable for touring cycling in most seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures, while winter can also be enjoyable with mild conditions. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Fuente Álamo De Murcia?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Fuente Álamo De Murcia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31416090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Águilas Marina – Road to Mazarrón loop from Las Palas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall of Mazarrón – Mazarrón Mines Historic Site loop from Las Palas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting places or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several points of interest. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3950819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt; historical site, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1462793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Cañar&lt;/a&gt; natural monument, and scenic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3067287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedacero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features traditional villages, country mansions, and windmills, reflecting its rural charm and history.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Fuente Álamo De Murcia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied topography, the rural scenery, and the proximity to the coast, which offers diverse riding experiences from greenways to challenging coastal roads.

Q: Are there any routes that offer coastal views or access to beaches?
A: Yes, Fuente Álamo De Murcia's proximity to the coast means several routes offer stunning coastal views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38236925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Plana Coastal View – Winding Road to Mazarrón loop from Mingrano&lt;/a&gt; take you through coastal areas, providing picturesque vistas and the chance to experience the sea breeze.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, some routes traverse or border parts of the Carrascoy National Park, offering opportunities to cycle through areas with pine forests and enjoy the natural environment. The region's diverse landscape includes mountain ranges like the Sierra del Algarrobo, providing varied natural settings for your ride.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on routes in Fuente Álamo De Murcia?
A: Elevation gain varies significantly across routes. Easier routes, such as greenways, have minimal climbing, while more challenging routes can feature substantial ascents. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall of Mazarrón – Mazarrón Mines Historic Site loop from Las Palas&lt;/a&gt; includes over 700 meters of elevation gain, providing a good workout.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths or greenways?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to greenways, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1457629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant and often flat cycling experience away from traffic. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017262305/via-verde-of-campo-de-cartagena-and-sierra-de-la-muela-cabo-tinoso-and-roldan-return-from-fuente-alamo-de-murcia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde of Campo de Cartagena and Sierra de la Muela&lt;/a&gt; utilize these historical paths.
tips: The VV is ok, but the surface is in places rough. However it only leads to a northern suburb of Cartagena. I have tried several times to go further but have only run into big roads with heavy traffic. Murcia/Cartagena need to join the VV with the other bike routes nearer the centre (preferably traffic free.)

Very very nice. We were here in May and everything was blooming, colorful and just beautiful.
Motorists proved to be very tolerant. So definitely get on the bike. 🚴👍

This regional park stands out for its wonderful gorges, steep hills and tiny coves with turquoise water. In addition, there are numerous roads and paths that go into the heart of the mountains, where you can visit the ruins of dismantled batteries, caves and watchtowers.
